skill.name: figma-automation
skill.description: Automate Figma tasks via Rube MCP (Composio): files, components, design tokens, comments, exports. Always search tools first for current schemas.
skill.summary: Automate Figma design workflows through Rube MCP with Composio's Figma toolkit for file access, component extraction, and asset export.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 86 patterns (52 external_commands, 33 cryptographic, 5 reconnaissance) but all are false positives. The SKILL.md file is documentation-only describing Rube MCP tool usage for Figma API interaction. No executable code, shell commands, or cryptographic operations exist. External API dependency via MCP warrants low risk classification.

low_findings.title: External API Dependency
low_findings.description: Skill depends on Rube MCP server (https://rube.app/mcp) for Figma API access. External service dependency introduces availability and trust considerations.

content.user_title: Automate Figma design workflows with AI
content.value_statement: Manual Figma tasks consume hours of repetitive work. This skill automates file extraction, component management, and asset export through Rube MCP integration.
actual_capabilities: Extract file data and components from Figma design files using FIGMA_GET_FILE_JSON and related tools
Render and download Figma nodes as PNG, SVG, JPG, or PDF images with configurable scale
Extract design tokens (colors, typography, spacing) and convert to Tailwind CSS configuration
Manage comments and version history in Figma files programmatically
Browse team projects and list published styles and components
Parse Figma URLs to extract file keys and node IDs for API operations
limitations: Only supports Figma Design files; FigJam boards and Slides are not supported
Requires active Rube MCP connection with Figma toolkit authorized
Team IDs must be extracted manually from Figma URLs, not programmatically
Rendered image URLs are temporary and expire after approximately 30 days

use_cases.title: Design System Documentation
use_cases.description: Extract components, styles, and design tokens from Figma files to generate living documentation for development teams.
use_cases.target_user: Design system leads maintaining cross-team consistency

use_cases.title: Asset Export Pipeline
use_cases.description: Automate batch export of design assets in multiple formats and scales for handoff to developers.

use_cases.title: Code Generation from Designs
use_cases.description: Extract design tokens and automatically generate Tailwind CSS configuration files for consistent styling.

prompt_templates.title: Extract Figma Components
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract all published components from this Figma file: [paste Figma URL]. List each component name and its variant properties.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner: Quick component inventory from a design file

prompt_templates.title: Export Design Assets
prompt_templates.prompt: Export all button components from this Figma file as PNG at 2x scale. Download the rendered images and save them to the assets folder.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate: Batch export specific component types

prompt_templates.title: Generate Tailwind Config from Design Tokens
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract design tokens from this Figma file and convert them to Tailwind CSS configuration. Include colors, typography, and spacing variables.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced: Full design token extraction and framework integration

prompt_templates.title: Comment Management Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: List all unresolved comments in this Figma file, then add a summary comment tagging the design team for review. Format the summary in markdown.

output_examples.output: Found 12 published components: Primary Button (4 variants), Input Field (3 variants), Card Container (2 variants), Navigation Bar, Modal Dialog, Dropdown Menu, Toast Notification, Avatar, Badge, Progress Bar, Tabs, Accordion

output_examples.input: Export the header component as PNG at 2x scale
output_examples.output: Rendered 1 node successfully. Download URL: https://figma-alpha-api.s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/images/abc123... (expires in 30 days). File saved to ./exports/header@2x.png

output_examples.input: Extract design tokens and convert to Tailwind config
output_examples.output: Extracted 24 color tokens, 8 typography scales, 12 spacing values. Generated tailwind.config.js with custom theme extending default config. Colors mapped to CSS variables for runtime theming.

best_practices: Always call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first to verify available Figma tools and current schemas before executing workflows
Use simplify=true parameter when fetching file data to reduce payload size by 70% while preserving essential information
Parse Figma URLs with FIGMA_DISCOVER_FIGMA_RESOURCES to correctly extract file keys and convert node ID formats
anti_patterns: Do not pass node IDs as arrays; the API requires comma-separated strings (e.g., '1:541,2:100' not ['1:541', '2:100'])
Avoid requesting broad node trees with high depth values; start with depth=2 for overview, then fetch specific nodes
Do not strip fields from design token extraction responses before passing to Tailwind conversion; full object structure is required

faq.question: What Figma file types are supported?
faq.answer: Only Figma Design files (figma.com/design/ or figma.com/file/) are supported. FigJam boards and Figma Slides return 400 errors from the API.

faq.question: How do I connect Rube MCP to Figma?
faq.answer: Add https://rube.app/mcp as an MCP server in your client configuration. Call RUBE_MANAGE_CONNECTIONS with toolkit 'figma' and follow the auth link if connection is not active.

faq.question: Why are my rendered image URLs expiring?
faq.answer: Figma provides temporary signed URLs valid for approximately 30 days. Download images promptly if you need permanent storage.

faq.question: What is the correct node ID format for the API?
faq.answer: Figma URLs use dash format (node-id=1-541) but the API requires colon format (1:541). Use FIGMA_DISCOVER_FIGMA_RESOURCES to handle conversion automatically.

faq.question: Can I export FigJam boards with this skill?
faq.answer: No. The FIGMA_GET_FILE_JSON endpoint only supports Design files. FigJam boards and Slides are not supported by the current API.

faq.question: How do I find my team ID for project listing?
faq.answer: Extract the team ID from your Figma URL: figma.com/files/team/TEAM_ID/project/... The team ID cannot be obtained programmatically through the API.
